title: windowsill pot.json
description: The sanitized snapshot the lab publishes and the /windowsill/ page consumes. Every field is optional so the page degrades to a procedural seed; this schema describes the shape when present. Shared by windowsill-lab (producer) and brokenbranchdevwebsite (consumer).

href.description: canonical report link emitted by the archive ledger; the page reads href, falling back to url

growth_form.description: Render-strategy hint derived from `track` (see BACKLOG.md §Growth forms): the *form* of the green thing — same pot, palette, and contract, only the form changes — so the kind of science is legible at a glance. Unknown/absent track falls back to the homogeneous default seedling.

result.description: the technical result string, lifted from the MILESTONES.md '(done … — …)' parenthetical

short_label.description: story layer (src/lab/stories.py): 2-4 plain words naming the experiment

question_plain.description: story layer: the experiment's question in everyday, jargon-free language

why_it_matters.description: story layer: one sentence on why a curious non-expert should care

result_plain.description: story layer: one plain-language sentence stating the verified outcome; null for open/pending

report.description: One run on record — a node on the seedling stem (verified) or a folded grey leaf (null / unscored / unreadable). All fields optional so old consumers degrade. The archive ledger emits {date, milestone, verdict, headline, href}; the older compact record adds {experiment, peak_t, wall_s, url, code_sha, status}.
